03bc38f6-1c91-414e-a5f5-e7ca3e38d9bf.title: Fix mobile kanban board blinking during agent streaming
03bc38f6-1c91-414e-a5f5-e7ca3e38d9bf.details:
## Root Cause

Each `card_progress` SSE event triggers a full app re-render through `renderKanbanEventPreservingScroll()` → `render()`. The `render()` function rebuilds all 4 DOM regions (left-nav, main, mobile-nav, overlays) via `innerHTML = html`, then re-binds every event listener. On mobile viewports (≤720px), this rapid DOM destruction/reconstruction causes visible blinking.

The existing `patchOpenCardProgress()` only patches when a card detail panel is open — it returns `false` for board-only views, causing a full render fallback.

## Changes

### File: `frontend/src/app/kanban/index.ts`

1. **Add `patchCardProgress(cardId: string, card: KanbanCard): boolean`**
   - Locate the single affected `<article.kanban-card>` by `data-card-id`
   - Update `.kanban-card-progress-bar` width/style and `.kanban-card-status-text` percentage inline
   - Return true if element found and patched, false otherwise

2. **Add `patchKanbanBoard(workspaceID: string): boolean`**
   - Locate `.kanban-panel` inside the main region
   - Use existing `patchChildrenFromHtml` / `morphChildren` infrastructure to update only the `.kanban-board` element (not the full app shell)
   - Update the panel-heading fragment (runtime label, button states) via targeted innerHTML replacement
   - Return true if elements found and patched, false otherwise

3. **Modify `applyKanbanEvent()` flow for `card_progress`:**
   ```
   Current:
     if (event.type === "card_progress" && patchOpenCardProgress(event)) return;
     renderKanbanEventPreservingScroll();  // full render

   New:
     if (event.type === "card_progress") {
       if (patchOpenCardProgress(event)) return;
       if (patchCardProgress(card.id, card)) return;      // single-element patch
       if (patchKanbanBoard(event.workspaceId)) return;    // board-level patch
     }
     renderKanbanEventPreservingScroll();  // fallback for structural changes
   ```

## Key constraints
- Reuse the existing `patchChildrenFromHtml` / `morphChildren` infrastructure — don't introduce a new diffing library.
- Preserve the full-render fallback for non-progress events (`card_status_changed`, `lane_moved`, etc.) since those are rare and may involve structural DOM changes.
- Ensure `bindEvents` is not called during incremental patches (it's expensive and causes listener duplication if old nodes linger).
- Verify on mobile viewport (≤720px) that the blinking disappears while card progress updates remain visually smooth.

## Testing
- Start a kanban run with streaming agent output on mobile viewport
- Confirm no visible DOM flicker/blinking during rapid `card_progress` events
- Confirm full re-render still works for structural changes (lane moves, status changes, board close/open)
acceptanceCriteria: No visible blinking on mobile viewport when agent streams kanban progress
Card progress bar and status text update smoothly without full DOM rebuild
Full render fallback still triggers for structural kanban events (status change, lane move, board open/close)
Existing patchOpenCardProgress behavior preserved when card detail panel is open
No regressions in desktop viewport rendering

2723673f-1be9-4dda-ae4a-c4d8113383e3.title: Task cards are too bulky and lack the visual style and flair of the dashboard mockup
2723673f-1be9-4dda-ae4a-c4d8113383e3.details:
## Problem
Task cards (in the backlog/workspace task board) are too bulky and don't match the compact, colorful, at-a-glance aesthetic demonstrated in the dashboard mockup. They need the same visual treatment as Kanban cards just received.

## Goal
Make task cards compact, visually expressive, and consistent with the dashboard mockup's design language so users can scan backlog status quickly without opening detail views.

## Dashboard Reference (`frontend/src/app/dashboard/index.ts`)
The dashboard shows task rows with:
- Priority badge (compact inline style)
- Title only (no description visible by default)
- Conversion status: "→ card ✓" for converted tasks, plain text for unconverted
- Clean spacing and minimal chrome

## Changes Needed

### Frontend (`frontend/src/app/workspace/tasks/index.ts`)
- `renderTask()`: Apply compact card layout matching dashboard task rows
- Show only priority badge, title, and conversion status by default
- Add color-coded status indicators:
  - Green "→ card ✓" for tasks converted to Kanban cards
  - Orange/yellow "ready" indicator for tasks eligible for sprint selection
  - Neutral "backlog" text for unconverted tasks
- Reduce padding/whitespace to decrease overall bulk

### CSS (`frontend/src/styles.css`)
- `.task-card`: Match dashboard task row styling (compact, tight spacing)
- Priority badges: Compact inline style with `--color-surface-overlay` background, small caps, tighter padding
- Status text color coding matching dashboard: green=converted, orange=ready, neutral=backlog
- Hover state: accent border tint, accent background tint, `translateY(-1px)` lift, `120ms ease`
- Reduce overall card padding to decrease bulk

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Task cards are visually compact (reduced padding/whitespace vs current)
2. Priority badges use dashboard-style compact inline format
3. Conversion status shown with color coding: green "→ card ✓", orange "ready", neutral "backlog"
4. Hover state has accent border tint, background tint, translateY(-1px) lift at 120ms ease
5. Visual style is consistent with dashboard mockup and Kanban cards
6. Dark/light theme compatible via existing CSS custom properties
7. Drag-and-drop behavior preserved (cursor: grab, .is-dragging opacity)
8. `cd frontend && npm run build` succeeds with no TypeScript errors
acceptanceCriteria: Task cards are visually compact (reduced padding/whitespace vs current)
Priority badges use dashboard-style compact inline format
Conversion status shown with color coding: green '→ card ✓', orange 'ready', neutral 'backlog'
Hover state has accent border tint, background tint, translateY(-1px) lift at 120ms ease
Visual style is consistent with dashboard mockup and Kanban cards
Dark/light theme compatible via existing CSS custom properties
Drag-and-drop behavior preserved (cursor: grab, .is-dragging opacity)
cd frontend && npm run build succeeds with no TypeScript errors

27e19655-5efe-48b2-a708-f72b4326abf8.title: Create mock-up dashboard view (non-functional)
27e19655-5efe-48b2-a708-f72b4326abf8.details:
## Goal

Build a visually complete but non-functional dashboard view with mock data so we can see what an elegant, modern Echo game dev studio looks like on desktop and mobile. This is purely exploratory — no backend wiring, no real events, just beautiful static UI.

## Design Direction

- **Single-screen command center**: Backlog (left), Kanban (right), Chat (bottom)
- **Glass cockpit aesthetic**: Minimal chrome, subtle status indicators, shared visual language across sections
- **Mobile-first responsive**: Stacks vertically on mobile, side-by-side on desktop (>720px breakpoint already used in app)
- **No redundant panels**: Shows cross-view state that individual views don't provide

## Mock Data (2.5D Roguelite Example)

### Status Bar
- Workspace: `roguelite-2d/`
- Heartbeat: active (● green dot, "5m interval")
- Watchdog: active (● green dot, "15m interval")  
- Budget: 14k / 50k tokens used
- Last build: ✓ passed

### Backlog Section (8 tasks)
| Priority | Title | Status |
|----------|-------|--------|
| P0 | Player movement & collision | → card ✓ |
| P0 | Dungeon room generation | → card ✓ |
| P1 | Combat system (turn-based) | → card ✓ |
| P1 | Enemy AI state machine | ready |
| P2 | Inventory & item drops | backlog |
| P2 | Death screen + run summary | backlog |
| P2 | Sound effects system | backlog |
| P2 | UI polish pass | backlog |

### Kanban Section
**Done (3 cards)**
- Player movement & collision — verification passed, 3 files changed
- Tile map loader — verification passed, 2 files changed  
- Camera system — verification passed, 1 file changed

**InProgress (1 card)**
- Dungeon room generation — agent running at 78%, last tool: filesystem_create_text

**Ready (2 cards)**
- Combat system — depends on movement (Done) ✓
- Procedural loot tables — depends on inventory (backlog) ⏳

**Blocked (0 cards)**

### Chat Section
Mock conversation showing task decomposition flow with Echo creating tasks from a feature description.

## Implementation Plan

### Frontend Files to Create/Modify
- `frontend/src/app/dashboard/index.ts` — Mock dashboard rendering function
- Add to existing render pipeline as a toggleable view mode (e.g., `?view=dashboard` or button)
- Reuse existing styling patterns from `styles.css` and theme system

### Visual Design Notes
- Use existing Echo color palette and typography
- Cards should look consistent between backlog and kanban sections
- Status indicators: green dot (done/active), yellow dot (running), blue dot (ready), red dot (blocked)
- Progress bar on in-progress cards showing agent completion percentage
- Subtle hover states for interactivity feel even though non-functional
- Mobile: single column stack, Kanban cards become horizontal scroll or accordion
- Desktop: two-column layout with backlog left, kanban right, chat full-width bottom

### Acceptance Criteria
- Dashboard view renders with all mock data visible
- Responsive layout works at 375px (mobile), 720px (tablet), and 1440px (desktop)
- No backend dependencies — all data is hardcoded mock objects
- Can be toggled on/off from existing UI (add button or URL param)
- Uses existing Echo theme system (dark/light mode compatible)
- Looks polished enough to use as design reference for future implementation

## Out of Scope
- Real task/kanban data binding
- Event subscriptions
- Task → card conversion functionality
- Chat interaction
- Backend API calls
acceptanceCriteria: - New file `frontend/src/app/dashboard/index.ts` exports `renderDashboard()` function that returns HTML string with all sections (status bar, backlog, kanban, chat)

- Mock data objects contain the 2.5D roguelite example: status bar with workspace name + heartbeat/watchdog/budget indicators, 8 backlog tasks with P0/P1/P2 priorities, kanban lanes showing Done(3)/InProgress(1)/Ready(2)/Blocked(0), mock chat conversation
- Dashboard view is accessible via URL parameter `?view=dashboard` or a toggle button added to existing UI navigation
- CSS media queries handle three breakpoints: 375px (single column stack), 720px (tablet adjustments), 1440px+ (two-column backlog/kanban side-by-side with full-width chat below)
- Status indicators use consistent color coding: green dot for done/active, yellow for running, blue for ready, red for blocked — matching existing Echo theme variables from `theme.ts`
- In-progress kanban card shows a visual progress bar at 78% with tool name label ("filesystem_create_text")
- Backlog tasks show conversion status: "→ card ✓" badge for converted tasks, plain text for unconverted
- Kanban cards in Ready lane show dependency status: green check for satisfied dependencies, orange hourglass for unsatisfied
- Dark and light theme both render correctly using existing Echo theme system (no hardcoded colors)
- `cd frontend && npm run build` succeeds with no TypeScript errors
- No imports of Wails bindings or backend services — dashboard is purely static mock HTML/CSS/TS
- Dashboard can be toggled away to return to normal Echo view without page reload

287c0c84-4965-43c1-a040-a19f3652c450.title: Task cards click opens detail view with action options (like kanban)

36146cb3-3e65-48c8-b76d-c790915945b4.title: Fix auto/heartbeat not picking up newly created Ready cards
36146cb3-3e65-48c8-b76d-c790915945b4.details:
## Problem
When Auto (heartbeat) is ON, newly created Ready cards are never picked up automatically. The user must manually click "Run" to trigger execution. Two root causes:

1. **Card creation blocked during active runs**: `CreateReadyKanbanCard` and `CreateKanbanCardFromChatMessage` reject cards when `kanbanRuns[workspaceID]` exists.
2. **No immediate scheduling on card creation**: After a scheduler run finishes (`forgetKanbanRun`), new Ready cards sit idle until the next heartbeat tick (could be 5-15 minutes).

## Changes

### Backend: `internal/services/kanban.go`

**`CreateReadyKanbanCard`** (line ~194):
- Remove the `chatMu.Lock()` block that checks for active `kanbanRuns[workspaceID]` and returns error. Cards should be creatable at any time.
- After creating the card, before returning: check if a kanban run is active. If NOT active, call `s.StartKanbanExecution(workspaceID, defaultAgentLimit)` to immediately start processing eligible cards. Ignore errors from this call (scheduler will just skip if something goes wrong).

**`CreateKanbanCardFromChatMessage`** (line ~148):
- Same pattern: remove the running-guard check, and auto-schedule after card creation if no run is active.

### Frontend: `frontend/src/app/kanban/index.ts`

**`handleCardCreationSubmit`** (around line 750):
- The call to `CreateReadyKanbanCard` may now succeed even during a run. Update board state from the returned value as before. If the backend returns an error about running, show it as a toast but don't crash.

### Frontend: `frontend/src/app/actions.ts`

**Chat-to-card handler** (around line 790):
- Same treatment — handle success during active runs gracefully.

### Testing
- Add/update tests in `internal/services/kanban_test.go`:
  - `TestCreateReadyKanbanCardDuringActiveRun` — verify card is created and scheduler picks it up
  - `TestCreateReadyKanbanCardAutoSchedulesWhenNoRun` — verify new run starts immediately
- Run `go test ./...` to validate.
acceptanceCriteria: Creating a Ready card while Auto is ON and no run active immediately starts the scheduler
Creating a Ready card while agents are running succeeds (no longer rejected)
Newly created cards are picked up by the running scheduler on its next iteration
Existing tests pass: go test ./...
Frontend board state updates correctly after card creation in all scenarios

538916a1-c373-4024-bf7b-5fbeeec640ee.title: Fix rebuild-relaunch.bat to capture PowerShell errors
538916a1-c373-4024-bf7b-5fbeeec640ee.details:
## Problem
The `.bat` launcher runs and self-deletes, but PowerShell errors flash in a hidden window and are lost. The user sees "The batch file cannot be found" (which just means it deleted itself) but never sees what actually failed.

## Fix
In `internal/services/rebuild_relaunch_windows.go`, change the bat content to redirect PowerShell stderr/stdout to a debug log:

**Current:**
```go
batContent := fmt.Sprintf("@powershell -NoProfile -NonInteractive -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File \\\"%s\\\"\\r\\ndel \\\"%s\\\"\\r\\n", scriptPath, batPath)
```

**Fix:**
```go
debugLog := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(scriptPath), "rebuild-relaunch-debug.log")
batContent := fmt.Sprintf("@powershell -NoProfile -NonInteractive -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File \\\"%s\\\" 2>&1 >> \\\"%s\\\"\\r\\ndel \\\"%s\\\"\\r\\n", scriptPath, debugLog, batPath)
```

This redirects all PowerShell output (including errors) to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Echo\rebuild-relaunch-debug.log` so the actual failure is visible.
acceptanceCriteria: 1. rebuild-relaunch.bat redirects PowerShell stderr/stdout to %LOCALAPPDATA%\Echo\rebuild-relaunch-debug.log via 2>&1 >> "debugLog" 2. Running Rebuild & Relaunch produces a populated debug log file with the actual PowerShell error (not just "batch file cannot be found") 3. go build ./... succeeds 4. go test ./internal/services/ -run TestPrepareRebuildAndRelaunch -v passes

54c33d77-e8be-45cd-9e9a-47b68281c090.title: Add dashboard layout state fields to state.ts

Add helper functions:

- `getDashboardWidgets(view: AppMode): DashboardWidget[]`
- `setDashboardWidgets(view: AppMode, widgets: DashboardWidget[])`

595b7c77-d8e9-45a4-b718-64e19aa6716a.title: Add task CRUD tooling to Echo
595b7c77-d8e9-45a4-b718-64e19aa6716a.details:
Add full task CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) capabilities to Echo so users can manage the backlog from within the app.

## Backend
- `CreateTask(title, priority, details?, acceptanceCriteria?)` → returns created task with ID
- `ListTasks(includeCompleted?)` → returns all tasks
- `UpdateTask(id, completed?, title?, priority?, details?, acceptanceCriteria?)` → partial update
- `DeleteTask(id)` → removes task

## Frontend
- Task management panel (Settings or standalone view)
- Create form: title, priority dropdown (P0/P1/P2), optional details/acceptance criteria
- List view: sortable by priority/completion, shows all fields
- Edit/delete actions per task
- Completion toggle

## Persistence
- Tasks stored in existing state.json alongside other Echo state
- Protected by SystemService.mu like other persisted state
acceptanceCriteria: Backend: Add exported methods on SystemService for CreateTask, ListTasks, UpdateTask (mark complete, edit fields), DeleteTask
Backend: Methods persist tasks to state.json alongside existing Echo state
Frontend: Add a task management UI section (e.g., in Settings or as a new panel) with create form, list view, and edit/delete actions
Frontend: Task list shows title, priority (P0/P1/P2), completed status, acceptance criteria, and details
Wails bindings regenerated after backend method additions
go build ./... succeeds
go test ./internal/services/ passes

77c517ec-9b26-476e-81a4-d3c0fb5e33c0.title: Add backend persistence for dashboard layouts

79383199-2626-44a9-bd5b-e3696aa2c679.title: Kanban progress numbers don't make sense - it stays on 99% for almost all of the implementation of the card
79383199-2626-44a9-bd5b-e3696aa2c679.details:
The Kanban card progress indicator jumps to 99% early and remains there for most of the card's execution. The progress calculation needs to be revisited so it reflects actual completion throughout the implementation phase rather than plateauing near the end prematurely.

**Likely areas to investigate:**
- `internal/services/kanban_scheduler.go` — card agent execution loop, progress reporting logic
- Progress event emission (`echo:kanban:event`) and how percentage is computed from tool call steps vs. total estimated work
- Frontend rendering of the progress value in `frontend/src/app/kanban/`

**Goal:** Progress should scale proportionally across the full card execution lifecycle, not front-load to ~99% before actual implementation completes.
acceptanceCriteria: Progress percentage reflects proportional completion throughout the entire card execution
Progress does not plateau at 99% during the main implementation phase
Final 1-2% is reserved for post-execution cleanup/verification steps

7de30adb-7b02-4ad9-a3e3-305e328352ed.title: Port minimal card styling from dashboard to Kanban and Task views
7de30adb-7b02-4ad9-a3e3-305e328352ed.details:
## Problem
Kanban cards are too large and show too much information. Dashboard mock-up has clean, minimal cards that should be ported to actual views.

## Changes Required

### Kanban Cards (`frontend/src/app/kanban/index.ts`)
- **Collapse card content**: Show only title, status dot/color, and progress percentage on the card itself
- **Hide from card view**: Description, full acceptance criteria, file changes, detailed progress log, dependency text
- **Click to expand**: Open existing card detail modal or inline expansion for full details
- **Style port from dashboard**:
  - Border radius: `8px` → `var(--space-lg)` (16px)
  - Background: `--color-surface` → `--color-surface-muted`
  - Hover: accent border tint + background tint + `translateY(-1px)` lift, `120ms ease`
  - Progress bar visible on card (like dashboard's `.dashboard-progress-bar`)

### Task Cards (`frontend/src/app/workspace/tasks/index.ts`)
- **Style port from dashboard**:
  - Border radius: `8px` → `var(--space-lg)` (16px)
  - Background: `--color-surface` → `--color-surface-muted`
  - Hover: accent border tint + background tint + `translateY(-1px)` lift, `120ms ease`
  - Keep existing shadow (`var(--shadow-soft)`)

### CSS Updates (`frontend/src/styles.css`)
- Update `.kanban-card` styles to match dashboard `.dashboard-kanban-card` minimal aesthetic
- Update `.task-card` hover states to match dashboard `.dashboard-task-row` hover behavior
- Ensure responsive at 375px / 720px / 1440px breakpoints

### Preserve
- Drag-and-drop behavior (`cursor: grab`, `.is-dragging` opacity)
- Lane headers with count badges
- Recovery/repair absolute-positioned overlays on kanban cards
- Existing card detail modal functionality
acceptanceCriteria: Kanban cards show only: title, status indicator (dot/color), progress percentage bar
Kanban cards hide: description, acceptance criteria, file changes, progress log, dependency text
Clicking a kanban card opens existing card detail modal with full information
.kanban-card uses border-radius: var(--space-lg) (16px)
.kanban-card uses background: var(--color-surface-muted)
.kanban-card hover has accent border tint + background tint + translateY(-1px) lift at 120ms ease
Progress bar visible on kanban card matching dashboard .dashboard-progress-bar styling
.task-card uses border-radius: var(--space-lg) (16px)
.task-card uses background: var(--color-surface-muted)
.task-card hover has accent border tint + background tint + translateY(-1px) lift at 120ms ease
Task cards preserve existing shadow (var(--shadow-soft))
Drag-and-drop behavior preserved (cursor: grab, .is-dragging opacity)
cd frontend && npm run build succeeds with no TypeScript errors
Responsive layout maintained at 375px / 720px / 1440px breakpoints

7e66abb8-eb33-4ca4-9559-d23c0d417a35.title: Replace dashboard widget grid with Dev Studio command center layout
7e66abb8-eb33-4ca4-9559-d23c0d417a35.details:
## Problem Dashboard renders individual small widget cards in a 4-column CSS grid. User wants a unified "Dev Studio" command center layout matching the mockup. ## Target Layout (3 sections) ### 1. Top Status Bar (full-width, compact single line) - Workspace name with green dot indicator - Heartbeat interval status (green dot + label like "5m interval") - Watchdog interval status (green dot + label like "15m interval") - Token budget: "Budget: 14k / 50k tokens" (with progress bar or color) - Last build status: "Last build: ✓ passed" ### 2. Two-Column Main Area (~60/40 split) **Left column — Backlog panel:** - Title "Backlog" - List of open tasks grouped by priority (P0, P1, P2) - Each task row: priority badge + title + status badge (→ card ✓ for converted, "ready", "backlog") - Show ~8-10 tasks max **Right column — Kanban panel:** - Title "Kanban" - 4 lanes horizontally: Done, In Progress, Ready, Blocked - Each lane shows count and cards with existing `.kanban-card` styling (reuse `renderKanbanCard` from kanban/index.ts) - Cards show title, status text, progress bar for inProgress ### 3. Full-Width Chat Section (bottom) - Title "Chat" - Last 4-6 messages from chat session rendered as compact bubbles - User messages right-aligned, assistant left-aligned - Truncated content (~140 chars per message) ## Files to Change ### `frontend/src/app/dashboard/grid.ts` — COMPLETE REWRITE of `renderDashboardWidgets()` Replace the widget grid with 3-section layout. Key imports needed: ```ts import { services } from "../../../wailsjs/go/models"; import { activeWorkspace, chatSessionFor, getActiveChatModelLabel, kanbanBoardFor, state, taskBoardFor } from "../state"; import { tokenBudgets, formatTokenCount, getBudgetProgress, getBudgetColorClass } from "../budget"; import { escapeHtml, escapeAttribute } from "../utils"; import { renderKanbanCard, laneDotClass } from "../kanban/index"; ``` New `renderDashboardWidgets(view)` structure: ```ts export function renderDashboardWidgets(view: AppMode): string { const workspace = activeWorkspace(); if (!workspace) return noWorkspaceHTML(); // existing empty state return `
${renderStatusBar(workspace)}
${renderBacklogPanel(workspace)} ${renderKanbanPanel(workspace)}
${renderChatSection(workspace)}
`; } ``` ### `frontend/src/styles.css` — Add new CSS classes (append after existing dashboard styles, ~line 8349) Key new selectors: - `.dashboard-studio` — flex column layout, gap, padding - `.dashboard-status-bar` — single-line flex row with status items, compact height (~40px), border bottom - `.dashboard-status-item` — inline-flex with dot + label - `.dashboard-studio-main` — CSS grid 2-column (1fr 2fr or ~35%/65%) - `.dashboard-backlog-panel` — panel container with title, scrollable task list - `.dashboard-kanban-panel` — panel container with kanban lanes grid - `.dashboard-chat-section` — full-width panel at bottom with message bubbles - Reuse existing `.priority-badge`, `.task-card`, `.kanban-card`, `.kanban-lane` styles where possible ### Keep intact: - Widget registry (`widgets.ts`) — still needed for other dashboard views (chat, tasks, kanban sub-views) - `index.ts` entry point — no change needed - Edit mode toggle can be removed or hidden for now since the new layout is fixed ## Data sources (already available in state): - Tasks: `taskBoardFor(workspace.id).tasks` - Kanban: `kanbanBoardFor(workspace.id)` with ready/inProgress/blocked/done arrays - Chat messages: `chatSessionFor(workspace.id).messages` - Heartbeat: `state.heartbeatIntervals.get(workspace.id)` - Watchdog: `state.watchdogIntervals.get(workspace.id)` - Token budget: `tokenBudgets.get(workspace.id)`
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard renders 3-section Dev Studio layout (status bar, backlog+kanban columns, chat section) instead of widget grid
Status bar shows workspace name, heartbeat interval, watchdog interval, token budget on single line
Backlog panel shows open tasks with priority badges and status indicators (converted/ready/backlog)
Kanban panel shows 4 lanes (Done, In Progress, Ready, Blocked) with cards using existing kanban-card styling
Chat section shows last 4-6 messages as compact bubbles with user right-aligned and assistant left-aligned
No workspace selected shows same empty-state message as before
Existing CSS variables and design tokens are used consistently (no hardcoded colors)
npm run build passes without errors in frontend
Other dashboard sub-views (chat, tasks, kanban views) still work if they use widget system

81e2e925-2184-4c5d-9a5b-828f700a31ab.title: Fix oversized SVG icons in Kanban card status text
81e2e925-2184-4c5d-9a5b-828f700a31ab.details:
## Problem Kanban cards on desktop show a giant black triangle shape inside Ready lane cards instead of compact status icons. The rendered layout is broken compared to the Dashboard card style, which shows clean rounded cards with small inline icons next to status text (e.g., ✓ ready, ✓ verification passed, ✗ blocked). ## Root Cause Inline SVG icons (`icons.check`, `icons.x`, hourglass emoji) inserted into `.kanban-card-status-text` paragraphs have **no CSS width/height constraints**. The rest of the stylesheet explicitly sizes every SVG container (e.g. `.recovery-badge svg { width: 10px; height: 10px; }`), but no such rule exists for status text SVGs. They render at their natural viewBox dimensions, appearing as oversized shapes that break the card layout. The `renderReadyStatus`, `renderDoneStatus`, `renderBlockedStatus`, and `renderInProgressStatus` functions in `frontend/src/app/kanban/index.ts` embed SVG icons directly into `

` elements: ```ts return `

${icons.check} ready

`; ``` ## Fix **File:** `echo/frontend/src/styles.css` (~line 2840, alongside existing `.kanban-card-status-text` rules) Add: ```css .kanban-card-status-text svg { flex: 0 0 auto; width: 14px; height: 14px; } ``` This mirrors the established pattern throughout the stylesheet where every SVG container gets explicit sizing. The `flex: 0 0 auto` prevents the SVG from shrinking or growing within the flex layout, and `14px` matches the visual scale of other status icons in the app (e.g. `.chat-more-menu button svg` at 14px). ## Acceptance Criteria - Ready lane cards render with a small green checkmark icon inline with "ready" text - Done lane cards show a small checkmark icon next to verification status text - Blocked lane cards show a small X icon next to block reason text - In Progress lane cards show compact percentage/status text without oversized shapes - All status icons are properly aligned with the text baseline inside the card - No layout overflow or clipping within Kanban cards on desktop viewport
acceptanceCriteria: Ready lane cards render with a small green checkmark icon inline with 'ready' text
Done lane cards show a small checkmark icon next to verification status text
Blocked lane cards show a small X icon next to block reason text
In Progress lane cards show compact percentage/status text without oversized shapes
All status icons are properly aligned with the text baseline inside the card
No layout overflow or clipping within Kanban cards on desktop viewport

841102ae-4010-4e04-98fc-0528751a1117.title: Add comprehensive CSS for all dashboard widget content classes

8ec98955-c733-4f34-a633-27f60c476225.title: Unify dashboard: ONE widget grid with ALL 15 widgets, remove per-view silos
8ec98955-c733-4f34-a633-27f60c476225.details:
## Problem
Dashboard is split into per-view silos. Clicking the dashboard button from chat shows only 4 chat widgets. From kanban it shows only 4 kanban widgets. The Dev Studio layout code in grid.ts **never renders** because `view === "dashboard"` never matches — `open-view-dashboard` sets `dashboardViewMode` to the current view ("chat", "kanban", etc.), so it always falls through to the filtered widget grid.

User wants: ONE unified dashboard where you pick ANY widget from ANY view, like an infotainment panel.

## Files to Change

### 1. `frontend/src/app/state.ts`
- **Remove** `dashboardViewMode` property (line 32) — no longer needed
- **Simplify** `defaultDashboardLayouts()`: ONE layout for "dashboard" with a good starting set of widgets from multiple views:
```ts
export function defaultDashboardLayouts(): Record<AppMode, DashboardWidget[]> {
  return {
    chat: [], tasks: [], kanban: [], code: [], git: [], settings: [],
    dashboard: [
      { id: "chat-busy-status", view: "dashboard", title: "Chat Status", size: "small", order: 0 },
      { id: "kanban-summary", view: "dashboard", title: "Kanban Summary", size: "wide", order: 1 },
      { id: "system-workspaces", view: "dashboard", title: "Workspaces", size: "medium", order: 2 },
      { id: "chat-token-budget", view: "dashboard", title: "Token Budget", size: "small", order: 3 },
      { id: "tasks-overview", view: "dashboard", title: "Tasks Overview", size: "medium", order: 4 },
      { id: "kanban-progress", view: "dashboard", title: "Card Progress", size: "medium", order: 5 },
      { id: "git-branch", view: "dashboard", title: "Current Branch", size: "small", order: 6 },
      { id: "chat-recent", view: "dashboard", title: "Recent Chat", size: "large", order: 7 },
      { id: "git-recent-commits", view: "dashboard", title: "Recent Commits", size: "medium", order: 8 },
      { id: "system-heartbeat", view: "dashboard", title: "Heartbeat", size: "small", order: 9 },
    ],
  };
}
```
- **Update** `getDashboardWidgets(view)` to always use `"dashboard"` as key (ignore the `view` param)
- **Update** `setDashboardWidgets(view)` similarly

### 2. `frontend/src/app/actions.ts`
- **Remove** `open-view-dashboard` action handler entirely (lines ~243-252)
- **Change** all widget CRUD actions that read `state.dashboardViewMode ?? "dashboard"` to just use `"dashboard"`:
  - `widget-remove`, `widget-add`, `widget-move-up`, `widget-move-down`
  - `reset-dashboard-layout`
  - `remove-widget`, `move-widget-up`, `move-widget-down` (the duplicate action names)

### 3. `frontend/src/app/dashboard/widgets.ts`
- **Remove** `availableWidgetsForView(view)` function — no longer needed
- Export a simple constant with ALL widget IDs:
```ts
export const allWidgetIds: WidgetId[] = Object.keys(widgetRegistry) as WidgetId[];
```

### 4. `frontend/src/app/dashboard/grid.ts`
- **Remove** the Dev Studio layout code (never rendered): `renderStatusBar`, `renderBacklogPanel`, `renderKanbanPanel`, `renderChatSection`, and all their helpers (`truncateText`, `formatIntervalMs`, `priorityBadgeHTML`, `statusDotHTML`, `renderTaskStatusBadge`, `renderDashboardKanbanCard`, `laneDotClass`, `kanbanCardProgressPercent`, `renderDashboardCardStatus`, `renderDashboardProgressBar`)
- **Simplify** `renderDashboardWidgets(view)` — remove the `view === "dashboard"` branch, always render the widget grid:
```ts
export function renderDashboardWidgets(): string {
  const workspace = activeWorkspace();
  if (!workspace) return noWorkspaceHTML();
  
  const widgets = getDashboardWidgets("dashboard");
  // ... existing widget card rendering logic (no change needed here)
}
```
- **Update** `renderAddWidgetPanel` to use `allWidgetIds` instead of `availableWidgetsForView(view)`

### 5. `frontend/src/app/dashboard/index.ts`
- Simplify to:
```ts
import { renderDashboardWidgets } from "./grid";

export function renderDashboard(): string {
  return renderDashboardWidgets();
}
```

### 6. `frontend/src/app/render.ts` — Side nav (line ~248)
- Change dashboard button from `open-view-dashboard` to `open-dashboard`:
```ts
<button class="nav-icon-button${mode === "dashboard" ? " is-active" : ""}" type="button" title="Dashboard" aria-label="Dashboard" data-action="${mode === "dashboard" ? "close-dashboard" : "open-dashboard"}">${icons.dashboard}</button>
```
- Remove the `data-view="${mode}"` attribute (no longer needed)
- Fix tooltip — remove `(state.dashboardViewMode ?? "chat")` reference

### 7. Remove per-view dashboard buttons from view headers:
These all have `open-view-dashboard` buttons that should be removed or changed to `open-dashboard`:
- **`frontend/src/app/chat/index.ts`** line ~558 — remove the `view-dashboard-button`
- **`frontend/src/app/git/index.ts`** line ~28 — remove the `view-dashboard-button`
- **`frontend/src/app/kanban/index.ts`** line ~1098 — remove the `view-dashboard-button`
- **`frontend/src/app/tasks/index.ts`** line ~77 — remove the `view-dashboard-button`
- **`frontend/src/app/render.ts`** line ~343 — remove the `view-dashboard-button` (inline kanban in code view)

### 8. `frontend/src/app/types.ts` and `frontend/src/app/grid.ts` imports
- Update `availableWidgets` export in grid.ts to use `allWidgetIds` instead of `availableWidgetsForView`
- Clean up any remaining references to `dashboardViewMode`

## Verification
1. Click dashboard button from ANY view → same unified widget grid appears
2. Customize mode shows ALL 15 widgets available for adding
3. Default layout has a good mix of widgets from chat, kanban, tasks, git, system views
4. `cd frontend && npm run build` succeeds with no TypeScript errors
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard button from ANY view opens the SAME unified dashboard
All 15 widgets are available to add/remove in customize mode
`open-view-dashboard` action removed from actions.ts
Per-view dashboard buttons in chat/git/kanban/tasks headers removed
Side nav dashboard button uses `open-dashboard` instead of `open-view-dashboard`
`dashboardViewMode` state property removed
`availableWidgetsForView` function removed or replaced with all-widget constant
Dev Studio layout code in grid.ts removed (never rendered anyway)
Default dashboard layout has widgets from multiple views (chat, kanban, tasks, git, system)
No TypeScript compilation errors — npm run build succeeds

95fc31f5-0a37-4b99-8f35-337fa151f64d.title: Redesign task cards to minimal compact view (matching kanban)
95fc31f5-0a37-4b99-8f35-337fa151f64d.details:
## Context
Kanban cards were just redesigned to show only title, status indicator, progress bar, and date — with details hidden until expand. Task cards need the same treatment for visual consistency.

## Changes Required

### CSS (styles.css)
- `.task-card`: increase border-radius to `var(--space-lg)`, background to `--color-surface-muted`
- `.task-card:hover`: accent border tint, accent background tint, `translateY(-1px)` lift, `120ms ease` transition
- `.task-card-header`: flex row with priority badge + title (no description visible)
- `.task-card-status-bar`: new class — horizontal bar showing status indicator dot + progress text (e.g., "converted ✓" or "backlog")
- `.task-card-body`: `max-height: 0`, `overflow: hidden`, `opacity: 0` — expands on click
- `.task-card.is-expanded .task-card-body`: `max-height: 500px`, `opacity: 1` with smooth transition
- Priority badges (`.priority-badge`): compact inline style matching dashboard (`--color-surface-overlay` background, small caps, tighter padding)
- Remove `.task-card-description` from default view — move into expandable body

### TypeScript (frontend/src/app/workspace/tasks/index.ts)
- `renderTask()`: strip description and acceptance criteria from default card HTML — only show priority badge, title, status indicator, progress text
- Add click handler on task card for expand/collapse toggling `.is-expanded` class
- Expanded view shows: full description, acceptance criteria checklist, conversion status badge, created date
- `renderTaskBoard()`: preserve drag-and-drop behavior on minimal cards

### TypeScript (frontend/src/app/workspace/tasks/tasks.ts)
- Update `moveTask()` and `setTaskPriority()` to work with compact card structure
- Ensure task detail panel still opens correctly when clicking expanded card content

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Task cards show only: priority badge, title, status indicator dot, progress text (e.g., "converted ✓" / "backlog")
2. Full description and acceptance criteria hidden by default in collapsed body
3. Click task card to expand/collapse with smooth `max-height` + `opacity` transition (200ms ease)
4. `.task-card` uses `border-radius: var(--space-lg)` and `background: --color-surface-muted`
5. Hover state: accent border tint, accent background tint, `translateY(-1px)` lift, `120ms ease` transition
6. Priority badges use compact inline style matching dashboard (`--color-surface-overlay` background)
7. Drag-and-drop behavior preserved on minimal cards (cursor: grab, is-dragging opacity)
8. Task detail panel still opens correctly when clicking expanded card content
9. Dark/light theme compatible via existing CSS custom properties
10. `cd frontend && npm run build` succeeds with no TypeScript errors
acceptanceCriteria: Task cards show only priority badge, title, status dot, and progress text by default
Full description and acceptance criteria hidden in collapsible body
Click to expand/collapse with smooth max-height + opacity transition (200ms)
border-radius: var(--space-lg), background: --color-surface-muted on .task-card
Hover: accent border tint, accent bg tint, translateY(-1px) lift, 120ms ease
Priority badges match dashboard compact inline style
Drag-and-drop behavior preserved (cursor: grab, is-dragging opacity)
Task detail panel opens correctly from expanded card content
Dark/light theme compatible via CSS custom properties
npm run build succeeds with no TypeScript errors

a14338ba-37de-4b71-851c-0c660e8d64fe.title: Set default layouts, polish empty states, and migrate away from mock-up
a14338ba-37de-4b71-851c-0c660e8d64fe.details:
**Files:** `frontend/src/app/state.ts`, `frontend/src/app/dashboard/widgets.ts`, `frontend/src/app/dashboard/grid.ts`

1. **Default layouts per view:**
   - Chat: chat-busy-status (small), chat-token-budget (wide), chat-recent (large), system-heartbeat (small)
   - Kanban: kanban-summary (wide), kanban-progress (medium), kanban-done-count (small), system-workspaces (small)
   - Tasks: tasks-overview (large), tasks-priority-strip (wide), system-heartbeat (small)
   - Git: git-branch (small), git-change-count (small), git-recent-commits (large)
   - Code: code-open-tabs (medium), code-workspace-status (small), system-heartbeat (small)

2. **Empty states** — When no workspace is selected, dashboard shows a helpful message instead of broken widgets.

3. **Remove mock-up data** — Delete hardcoded mock data from `frontend/src/app/dashboard/index.ts` once live widgets are verified working.

4. **npm run build** passes cleanly after all changes.
acceptanceCriteria: All 5 views have sensible default layouts
No workspace selected shows empty state message
Mock data is removed from dashboard/index.ts
npm run build passes with zero errors
go test ./... passes

a38b945f-4f6c-4b39-a294-55ac3f2584bc.title: Unify dashboard: remove per-view silos, make ONE widget grid with ALL 15 widgets
a38b945f-4f6c-4b39-a294-55ac3f2584bc.details:
## Problem
The dashboard is split into per-view silos. Clicking the dashboard button from chat shows only 4 chat widgets. From kanban it shows only 4 kanban widgets. This is backwards — you want ONE unified dashboard where you pick ANY widget from ANY view, like an infotainment panel.

## Root Cause
1. `open-view-dashboard` action sets `state.dashboardViewMode = currentView`, so `renderDashboardWidgets(view)` receives "chat", "kanban", etc. instead of "dashboard"
2. `availableWidgetsForView(view)` in `widgets.ts` filters widgets by view (e.g., chat only gets 4)
3. The Dev Studio layout code in `grid.ts` **never renders** because `view === "dashboard"` never matches — it always falls through to the widget grid with filtered widgets

## Changes Required

### 1. `frontend/src/app/actions.ts`
- **Remove** `open-view-dashboard` action (lines ~243-252)
- Keep `open-dashboard` as-is (it already sets `appMode = "dashboard"` correctly)
- Update all widget CRUD actions to always use `"dashboard"` as the view key instead of `state.dashboardViewMode`:
  - `widget-remove`, `widget-add`, `widget-move-up`, `widget-move-down`, `reset-dashboard-layout`, `remove-widget`, `move-widget-up`, `move-widget-down` — change `const view = state.dashboardViewMode ?? "dashboard"` to just `const view: AppMode = "dashboard"`

### 2. `frontend/src/app/state.ts`
- **Remove** `dashboardViewMode` from AppState (line ~32)
- **Simplify** `defaultDashboardLayouts()` — remove per-view layouts, have ONE layout for `"dashboard"` containing ALL 15 widgets:
  ```ts
  export function defaultDashboardLayouts(): Record<AppMode, DashboardWidget[]> {
    return {
      chat: [],
      tasks: [],
      kanban: [],
      code: [],
      git: [],
      dashboard: [
        { id: "chat-busy-status", view: "dashboard", title: "Chat Status", size: "small", order: 0 },
        { id: "kanban-summary", view: "dashboard", title: "Kanban Summary", size: "wide", order: 1 },
        { id: "system-workspaces", view: "dashboard", title: "Workspaces", size: "medium", order: 2 },
        { id: "chat-token-budget", view: "dashboard", title: "Token Budget", size: "small", order: 3 },
        { id: "tasks-overview", view: "dashboard", title: "Tasks Overview", size: "medium", order: 4 },
        { id: "kanban-progress", view: "dashboard", title: "Card Progress", size: "medium", order: 5 },
        { id: "git-branch", view: "dashboard", title: "Current Branch", size: "small", order: 6 },
        { id: "chat-recent", view: "dashboard", title: "Recent Chat", size: "large", order: 7 },
        { id: "git-recent-commits", view: "dashboard", title: "Recent Commits", size: "medium", order: 8 },
        { id: "system-heartbeat", view: "dashboard", title: "Heartbeat", size: "small", order: 9 },
      ],
      settings: [],
    };
  }
  ```
- Update `getDashboardWidgets` to always use `"dashboard"` as the key
- Update `setDashboardWidgets` similarly

### 3. `frontend/src/app/dashboard/widgets.ts`
- **Remove** `availableWidgetsForView(view)` — it's no longer needed since all widgets are available on the unified dashboard
- OR simplify it to return ALL 15 widget IDs for any view (since they're all available now)

### 4. `frontend/src/app/dashboard/grid.ts`
- Simplify `renderDashboardWidgets(view)` — remove the per-view branch logic, always render the unified widget grid with customize mode
- Remove the Dev Studio layout code (`renderStatusBar`, `renderBacklogPanel`, `renderKanbanPanel`, `renderChatSection`) since it never rendered anyway and isn't what the user wants
- The main view should be the widget grid with:
  - Toolbar with "Dashboard" title + Customize button
  - Widget grid showing configured widgets
  - Add Widget panel in customize mode showing ALL available widgets minus those already added

### 5. `frontend/src/app/dashboard/index.ts`
- Simplify to always pass `"dashboard"` as the view:
  ```ts
  export function renderDashboard(): string {
    return renderDashboardWidgets("dashboard");
  }
  ```

### 6. `frontend/src/app/render.ts` — Side nav dashboard button (line ~248)
- Change from `open-view-dashboard` to `open-dashboard`:
  ```ts
  <button class="nav-icon-button${mode === "dashboard" ? " is-active" : ""}" type="button" title="Dashboard" aria-label="Dashboard" data-action="${mode === "dashboard" ? "close-dashboard" : "open-dashboard"}">${icons.dashboard}</button>
  ```

### 7. Remove per-view dashboard buttons from view headers:
These files have `open-view-dashboard` buttons that need to be removed or changed to `open-dashboard`:
- **`frontend/src/app/chat/index.ts`** line ~558: Remove the `view-dashboard-button` from chat header (or change to `open-dashboard`)
- **`frontend/src/app/git/index.ts`** line ~28: Same
- **`frontend/src/app/kanban/index.ts`** line ~1098: Same
- **`frontend/src/app/tasks/index.ts`** line ~77: Same
- **`frontend/src/app/render.ts`** line ~343: Same (inline kanban in code view)

### 8. `frontend/src/app/types.ts` — Verify `DashboardWidget` type doesn't need changes (it has a `view` property that can be dropped or kept as "dashboard")

## Widget Options Concept
The user wants widgets to have options (e.g., kanban widget shows only in-progress cards, or all cards). This is a future enhancement — the immediate fix is making ONE unified dashboard with ALL widgets available. Widget options can be a follow-up task.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard button from ANY view opens the SAME unified dashboard
All 15 widgets are available to add/remove in customize mode
No per-view widget filtering — `availableWidgetsForView` returns all widgets or is removed
`open-view-dashboard` action is removed from actions.ts
Per-view dashboard buttons in chat/git/kanban/tasks headers are changed to `open-dashboard` or removed
Side nav dashboard button uses `open-dashboard` instead of `open-view-dashboard`
`dashboardViewMode` state property is removed
Default dashboard layout includes a good starting set of widgets from multiple views
npm run build succeeds in frontend

a3e8c3e8-fb41-40af-a280-fb29beb42d42.title: Wire dashboard actions and per-view entry points
a3e8c3e8-fb41-40af-a280-fb29beb42d42.details:
**Modify:** `frontend/src/app/actions.ts`

Add action handlers:
- `dashboard-edit-toggle` — Toggle `state.dashboardEditMode`
- `add-widget` — Add widget to current view's layout (read `data-widget-id`)
- `remove-widget` — Remove widget from layout
- `move-widget-up` / `move-widget-down` — Reorder widgets in layout array
- `reset-dashboard-layout` — Restore defaults for current `dashboardViewMode`
- `open-view-dashboard` — Set `dashboardViewMode` to current view, switch to dashboard mode

**Modify:** `frontend/src/app/render.ts`

- Update `buildMain()` to pass workspace to `renderDashboardWidgets(state.dashboardViewMode ?? "chat")`
- Add per-view dashboard entry: each view header or left nav gets a toggle button to open that view's dashboard
- Update `buildLeftNav()` dashboard button behavior for per-view mode
acceptanceCriteria: All new actions are handled in handleAction() without falling through
Opening a view's dashboard preserves the previous view for return navigation
Adding/removing widgets immediately re-renders the grid
Reset restores default layout for the active view

afc60737-2864-4f5f-be52-071d77579ded.title: Kanban card should show progress bar as well as active tool call, like the dashboard mockup has
afc60737-2864-4f5f-be52-071d77579ded.details:
## Problem
Kanban cards currently don't show a visual progress bar or the active tool call being executed. The dashboard mockup demonstrates both of these elements on in-progress cards and they should be ported to the actual Kanban view.

## Dashboard Mockup Reference
In the dashboard (`frontend/src/app/dashboard/index.ts`), in-progress kanban cards show:
- A visual progress bar at a percentage (e.g., 78%)
- The last tool name being executed (e.g., `filesystem_create_text`)

## Changes Needed

### Frontend (`frontend/src/app/kanban/index.ts`)
- Add a mini progress bar to each in-progress kanban card showing the current completion percentage
- Display the active/last tool call name alongside or below the progress bar
- Match the dashboard mockup's visual style for consistency

### CSS (`frontend/src/styles.css`)
- Style the inline progress bar on kanban cards (can likely reuse `.dashboard-progress-bar` or similar patterns)
- Tool call label styling to match dashboard aesthetic

## Acceptance Criteria
1. In-progress Kanban cards display a visual progress bar showing percentage completion
2. Active/last tool call name is visible on in-progress cards
3. Visual style matches the dashboard mockup's in-progress card rendering
4. Non-in-progress cards (Done, Ready, Blocked) don't show the progress bar/tool call or show them in an appropriate disabled state
5. `cd frontend && npm run build` succeeds with no TypeScript errors
acceptanceCriteria: In-progress Kanban cards display a visual progress bar showing percentage completion
Active/last tool call name is visible on in-progress cards
Visual style matches the dashboard mockup's in-progress card rendering
Non-in-progress cards handle progress bar/tool call appropriately (hidden or disabled)
cd frontend && npm run build succeeds with no TypeScript errors

b7fbe425-f1b9-4725-a018-c08bca046a64.title: Kanban cards are too bulky and lack the colorful, at-a-glance status flair of the dashboard mockup
b7fbe425-f1b9-4725-a018-c08bca046a64.details:
## Problem
Kanban cards are too bulky and don't convey important status information at a glance. The dashboard mockup demonstrates a much more polished, color-rich approach with contextual status messages that should be ported to the actual Kanban view.

## Goal
Make Kanban cards compact and visually expressive so users can understand card state without opening the detail view. Every lane's cards should have meaningful color-coded status text.

## Dashboard Reference (`frontend/src/app/dashboard/index.ts`)

### Done Lane Cards
- Green check mark (✓) with green text: "verification passed, 3 files changed"
- Shows file change count as a concrete success signal

### InProgress Lane Cards
- Yellow/amber indicator dot
- Progress bar showing percentage (e.g., 78%)
- Last tool name label (e.g., `filesystem_create_text`)

### Ready Lane Cards
- Blue indicator dot
- Green check with green text: "depends on movement (Done) ✓" when dependencies are met
- Orange hourglass (⏳) with orange text: "depends on inventory (backlog)" when dependencies are unmet — names the specific blocking dependency

### Blocked Lane Cards
- Red indicator dot
- Red text explaining why it was blocked (e.g., "Agent stopped early", error message excerpt)

## Changes Needed

### Frontend (`frontend/src/app/kanban/index.ts`)
- `renderKanbanCard()`: Add contextual status messages below the card title for each lane:
  - **Done**: Green check + "verification passed, N files changed" (pull from card verification result and file change count)
  - **InProgress**: Active tool call name + progress percentage (already discussed in separate task, but should integrate here)
  - **Ready**: Dependency status — green text with check if all deps met, orange text naming specific unmet dependency if not
  - **Blocked**: Red text showing the block reason/recovery message
- Compact overall card layout to reduce bulk

### CSS (`frontend/src/styles.css`)
- Color-coded status text classes matching dashboard:
  - Green for success/completed states
  - Orange/amber for waiting/pending states
  - Red for blocked/error states
  - Blue for ready states
- Smaller, tighter card padding and typography to reduce bulk
- Status message font size smaller than title, with appropriate color variables

### Data Availability Check
Verify that the following data is available on the `KanbanCard` struct or accessible from the frontend state:
- Verification result (passed/failed) and file change count for Done cards
- Current tool call name and progress percentage for InProgress cards
- Dependency list and their current lane status for Ready cards
- Block reason/recovery message for Blocked cards

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Done cards show green check + "verification passed, N files changed" text
2. InProgress cards show active tool call name and progress percentage
3. Ready cards show dependency status: green text with ✓ if met, orange text naming specific unmet dependency
4. Blocked cards show red text explaining the block reason
5. Cards are visually compact compared to current rendering (reduced padding/whitespace)
6. Color coding is consistent: green=success, orange=waiting, red=error, blue=ready
7. Dark/light theme compatible via existing CSS custom properties
8. `cd frontend && npm run build` succeeds with no TypeScript errors
acceptanceCriteria: Done cards show green check + 'verification passed, N files changed' text
InProgress cards show active tool call name and progress percentage
Ready cards show dependency status: green text with check if met, orange text naming specific unmet dependency
Blocked cards show red text explaining the block reason
Cards are visually compact compared to current rendering (reduced padding/whitespace)
Color coding is consistent: green=success, orange=waiting, red=error, blue=ready
Dark/light theme compatible via existing CSS custom properties
cd frontend && npm run build succeeds with no TypeScript errors

c655e011-d78a-44be-ad54-299c69ece8b1.title: Redesign dashboard from widget grid to Dev Studio command center layout
c655e011-d78a-44be-ad54-299c69ece8b1.details:
## Problem

The current dashboard renders individual widget cards in a CSS grid (Heartbeat, Tasks Overview, Priority Strip, Kanban Summary as separate boxes). The target is a unified "Dev Studio" command center with integrated backlog, kanban lanes, and chat sections.

## Current State (Image 1)

- `.dashboard-view` → `.dashboard-widget-grid` of individual `<article.widget-card>` elements
- Each widget card has a header title + body content
- Widgets: Heartbeat (small), Tasks Overview (large), Priority Strip (wide), Kanban Summary (small)
- No visual connection between backlog tasks and kanban cards

## Target State (Image 2)

Single-page command center with these sections:

### Top Status Bar
- Workspace name + path
- Heartbeat interval indicator (green dot + "5m interval")
- Watchdog interval indicator (green dot + "15m interval")
- Token budget ("Budget: 14k / 50k tokens")
- Last build status ("Last build: ✓ passed")

### Two-Column Main Area

**Left: Backlog Panel**
- Title "Backlog"
- List of tasks with priority badges (P0, P1, P2)
- Conversion status on right side: "→ card ✓" (green), "ready" (blue), "backlog" (gray)
- Tasks sourced from `taskBoardFor(workspace.id).tasks`

**Right: Kanban Panel**
- Title "Kanban"
- Lane headers with counts: DONE (3), IN PROGRESS (1), READY (2), BLOCKED (0)
- Cards in each lane showing title + status text (reuse existing kanban card rendering)
- Done cards: "verification passed · N files changed"
- In Progress cards: progress bar + tool name
- Ready cards: dependency status with check/hourglass icons

### Full-Width Chat Section
- Title "Chat"
- Last few messages from active chat session
- User messages on right (dark blue bubbles), Echo messages on left (gray bubbles)

## Implementation Plan

### File: `frontend/src/app/dashboard/index.ts`

Replace current widget grid rendering with the new unified layout:

1. **Remove** delegation to `renderDashboardWidgets()` for the main dashboard view
2. **Add** `renderCommandCenter(workspace: Workspace): string` that builds:
   - Status bar section
   - Two-column backlog + kanban section
   - Chat history section
3. **Keep** existing widget grid as fallback/editable layout option

### File: `frontend/src/styles.css`

Add new CSS classes for the command center layout:

```css
.dashboard-command-center { /* main container */ }
.dashboard-status-bar { /* top bar with indicators */ }
.dashboard-status-indicator { /* individual indicator items */ }
.dashboard-main-grid { /* two-column layout */ }
.dashboard-backlog-panel { /* left panel */ }
.dashboard-kanban-panel { /* right panel */ }
.dashboard-chat-section { /* bottom full-width section */ }
.backlog-task-row { /* individual task row with priority badge + status */ }
.backlog-priority-badge { /* P0/P1/P2 badges */ }
.backlog-status-badge { /* card ✓, ready, backlog badges */ }
```

### Data Sources (already available in state)

- `taskBoardFor(workspace.id)` → tasks for backlog panel
- `kanbanBoardFor(workspace.id)` → lanes/cards for kanban panel  
- `chatSessionFor(workspace.id)` → messages for chat section
- `state.heartbeatIntervals`, `state.watchdogIntervals` → status bar
- `tokenBudgets.get(workspace.id)` → budget display
- Reuse `renderKanbanCard()` and related functions from `./kanban/index.ts`

### Reuse Existing Functions

- `renderKanbanCard()` — kanban card HTML
- `renderKanbanCardStatus()` — status text per lane
- `kanbanCardProgressPercent()` — progress percentage
- `priorityBadgeHTML()` from widgets.ts — priority badges
- `statusDotHTML()` from widgets.ts — heartbeat/watchdog dots

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Dashboard shows unified command center layout (not widget grid)
2. Top status bar displays workspace, heartbeat, watchdog, budget indicators
3. Backlog panel lists tasks with priority badges and conversion status
4. Kanban panel shows lanes with cards matching existing kanban view style
5. Chat section shows recent conversation messages
6. Responsive layout stacks vertically on mobile (≤720px)
7. Dark/light theme compatible via CSS custom properties
8. `cd frontend && npm run build` succeeds with no TypeScript errors
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard shows unified command center layout (not widget grid)
Top status bar displays workspace, heartbeat, watchdog, budget indicators
Backlog panel lists tasks with priority badges and conversion status
Kanban panel shows lanes with cards matching existing kanban view style
Chat section shows recent conversation messages
Responsive layout stacks vertically on mobile (≤720px)
Dark/light theme compatible via CSS custom properties
cd frontend && npm run build succeeds with no TypeScript errors

ccc8b71b-2cc5-4ac5-97ec-bdfa46f1d1cc.title: Port dashboard card styling to Kanban and Task views
ccc8b71b-2cc5-4ac5-97ec-bdfa46f1d1cc.details:
## Changes

### `.kanban-card` (styles.css ~line 2339)
- `border-radius`: `8px` → `var(--space-lg)` (16px)
- `background`: `--color-surface` → `--color-surface-muted`
- Transition timing: `160ms ease` → `120ms ease` to match dashboard

### `.task-card` (styles.css ~line 2114)
- `border-radius`: `8px` → `var(--space-lg)` (16px)
- `background`: `--color-surface` → `--color-surface-muted`
- **Add** hover state: accent border tint, accent background tint, `translateY(-1px)` lift, `120ms ease` transition

### Preserve
- `.task-card` shadow (`var(--shadow-soft)`) — adds depth dashboard lacks
- Task drag behavior (`cursor: grab`, `.is-dragging` opacity)
- Recovery/repair badges on kanban cards (absolute overlays)
- Lane headers, count badges, priority text — no conflict

### Verification
- `cd frontend && npm run build` succeeds
- Dark/light theme both render correctly (uses CSS custom properties)
acceptanceCriteria: .kanban-card border-radius changed to var(--space-lg), background changed to --color-surface-muted, transition timing 120ms
.task-card border-radius changed to var(--space-lg), background changed to --color-surface-muted
.task-card hover state added with accent border tint, accent background tint, translateY(-1px) lift, 120ms ease transition
Task card drag behavior (cursor: grab, .is-dragging opacity) preserved
Recovery/repair badges on kanban cards preserved
cd frontend && npm run build succeeds

f5c28cde-e9e8-4767-b61c-7e9afd96cf85.title: Create widget registry with live data renderers
f5c28cde-e9e8-4767-b61c-7e9afd96cf85.details:
**New file:** `frontend/src/app/dashboard/widgets.ts`

Implement the widget registry — a map of WidgetId to renderer functions that read from live `state.*` and return HTML strings. Each widget must:
- Accept `(workspace: services.Workspace | null)` and return HTML string
- Use CSS custom properties (no hardcoded colors)
- Handle empty/null data gracefully

**Widgets to implement:**
- `chat-recent` — Last 3 messages from active chat session
- `chat-busy-status` — Busy/idle indicator + current model label
- `chat-token-budget` — Token budget bar (reuse existing budget logic)
- `kanban-summary` — Lane counts as compact badges
- `kanban-progress` — Active card progress bars for inProgress lane
- `kanban-done-count` — Done cards count with trend indicator
- `tasks-overview` — Open/completed counts + top P0 items
- `tasks-priority-strip` — Horizontal priority badges row
- `git-branch` — Current branch name + commit info
- `git-recent-commits` — Last 5 commits as compact list
- `git-change-count` — Uncommitted changes badge
- `system-heartbeat` — Heartbeat/watchdog interval status
- `system-workspaces` — Active workspace list with status dots

Export `widgetRegistry: Record<WidgetId, { renderer, defaultSize, title }>` and `availableWidgetsForView(view: AppMode): WidgetId[]`.
acceptanceCriteria: All 13+ widget renderers compile and return valid HTML
No widget imports Wails bindings or backend services directly (read from state only)
Empty data states show sensible placeholders, not errors
widgetRegistry exports match all WidgetId values
